title: Plan a Stage 2 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 2 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 2 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 2 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Digit” mean?
options: A single symbol from 0 to 9
What a digit is worth from its position
The two places in a two-digit number
A sensible guess before counting
questions.answer: A single symbol from 0 to 9
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digit means A single symbol from 0 to 9.

questions.question: What does “Place value” mean?
options: What a digit is worth from its position
The two places in a two-digit number
A sensible guess before counting
Numbers following a pattern
questions.answer: What a digit is worth from its position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Place value means What a digit is worth from its position.

questions.question: What does “3D shape” mean?
options: A solid shape you can hold
Flat surface / line join / corner point
A rounded surface that is not flat
A closed 2D shape with straight sides
questions.answer: A solid shape you can hold
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 3D shape means A solid shape you can hold.

questions.question: What does “Face / Edge / Vertex” mean?
options: Flat surface / line join / corner point
A rounded surface that is not flat
A closed 2D shape with straight sides
5 sides / 6 sides / 8 sides
questions.answer: Flat surface / line join / corner point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Face / Edge / Vertex means Flat surface / line join / corner point.

questions.question: A line on Farah's page runs from the 2 mark to the 10 mark on her ruler. How long is the line?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 3 methods.
questions.explanation: The line does not start at 0, so we find the difference. Take the start away from the end: 10 − 2 = 8 centimetres. The line is 8 cm long, not 10 cm.

questions.question: A rope is 24 metres long. Yusuf cuts off 14 metres. How much rope is left?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 3 methods.
questions.explanation: The rope that is left is the difference between what he started with and what he cut off. 24 − 14 = 10 metres. There are 10 metres of rope left.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information we collect
To put things into groups by a rule
A grid that sorts by whether items have features
The rules used to sort (one or two at a time)
questions.answer: Information we collect
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information we collect.

questions.question: What does “Sort” mean?
options: To put things into groups by a rule
A grid that sorts by whether items have features
The rules used to sort (one or two at a time)
A single line standing for one thing
questions.answer: To put things into groups by a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sort means To put things into groups by a rule.

questions.question: Work out 56 + 7.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 5 methods.
questions.explanation: Start at 56. To reach the next ten, 60, we need 4. Split the 7 into 4 and 3. Add the 4 first: 56 + 4 = 60. Then add the remaining 3: 60 + 3 = 63. So 56 + 7 = 63.

questions.question: Work out 38 + 30.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 5 methods.
questions.explanation: Adding tens leaves the ones alone. The ones digit stays 8. Add the tens: 3 tens + 3 tens = 6 tens. So the answer is 6 tens and 8 ones, which is 68. So 38 + 30 = 68.

questions.question: What does “Currency” mean?
options: The kind of money a country uses
Metal money / paper money
How much a coin or note is worth
The answer when you add amounts
questions.answer: The kind of money a country uses
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Currency means The kind of money a country uses.

questions.question: What does “Coin / Note” mean?
options: Metal money / paper money
How much a coin or note is worth
The answer when you add amounts
Money you get back when you overpay
questions.answer: Metal money / paper money
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coin / Note means Metal money / paper money.

questions.question: What does “Second / Minute / Hour” mean?
options: The short units: 60 seconds = 1 minute, 60 minutes = 1 hour
24 hours = 1 day; 7 days = 1 week
About 4 weeks = 1 month; 12 months = 1 year
A chart of days, weeks and months
questions.answer: The short units: 60 seconds = 1 minute, 60 minutes = 1 hour
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Second / Minute / Hour means The short units: 60 seconds = 1 minute, 60 minutes = 1 hour.

questions.question: What does “Day / Week” mean?
options: 24 hours = 1 day; 7 days = 1 week
About 4 weeks = 1 month; 12 months = 1 year
A chart of days, weeks and months
A day named by number, month and year
questions.answer: 24 hours = 1 day; 7 days = 1 week
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Day / Week means 24 hours = 1 day; 7 days = 1 week.

questions.question: What does “Number word” mean?
options: A number written in letters, like forty-two
The dash joining the tens word to the ones word
Change a number to the nearest ten
The multiple of ten a number is closest to
questions.answer: A number written in letters, like forty-two
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Number word means A number written in letters, like forty-two.

questions.question: What does “Hyphen” mean?
options: The dash joining the tens word to the ones word
Change a number to the nearest ten
The multiple of ten a number is closest to
Break a number into tens and ones a new way
questions.answer: The dash joining the tens word to the ones word
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Hyphen means The dash joining the tens word to the ones word.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information we collect
Asking people a question to gather data
A line used to count one item
How many times something happens
questions.answer: Information we collect
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information we collect.

questions.question: What does “Survey” mean?
options: Asking people a question to gather data
A line used to count one item
How many times something happens
Overlapping circles that sort items
questions.answer: Asking people a question to gather data
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Survey means Asking people a question to gather data.

questions.question: Work out 42 + 36.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 10 methods.
questions.explanation: Partition both numbers. 42 = 40 + 2 and 36 = 30 + 6. Add the tens: 40 + 30 = 70. Add the ones: 2 + 6 = 8. Join them back together: 70 + 8 = 78.

questions.question: Work out 38 + 27.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 10 methods.
questions.explanation: Partition: 38 = 30 + 8 and 27 = 20 + 7. Tens: 30 + 20 = 50. Ones: 8 + 7 = 15. But 15 is one ten and five ones, so pass the ten across: 50 + 15 = 50 + 10 + 5 = 65.

questions.question: What does “Turn” mean?
options: A movement that changes the direction you face
The way clock hands move / the opposite way
One fourth of a full turn; makes a right angle
Two quarter turns; makes a straight angle
questions.answer: A movement that changes the direction you face
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Turn means A movement that changes the direction you face.

questions.question: What does “Clockwise / Anticlockwise” mean?
options: The way clock hands move / the opposite way
One fourth of a full turn; makes a right angle
Two quarter turns; makes a straight angle
A complete turn back to the start; four quarter turns
questions.answer: The way clock hands move / the opposite way
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Clockwise / Anticlockwise means The way clock hands move / the opposite way.

questions.question: What does “Analog clock” mean?
options: A round clock with hands
A clock that shows numbers, like 7:30
The short, thick hand
The long, thin hand
questions.answer: A round clock with hands
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Analog clock means A round clock with hands.

questions.question: What does “Digital clock” mean?
options: A clock that shows numbers, like 7:30
The short, thick hand
The long, thin hand
The minute hand is on 12; a whole hour
questions.answer: A clock that shows numbers, like 7:30
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digital clock means A clock that shows numbers, like 7:30.

questions.question: What does “Mass” mean?
options: How heavy or light an object is
A tool that compares two masses; heavier side sinks
Units of mass (1 kg = 1000 g)
How hot or cold something is
questions.answer: How heavy or light an object is
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Mass means How heavy or light an object is.

questions.question: What does “Balance scale” mean?
options: A tool that compares two masses; heavier side sinks
Units of mass (1 kg = 1000 g)
How hot or cold something is
The unit of temperature
questions.answer: A tool that compares two masses; heavier side sinks
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Balance scale means A tool that compares two masses; heavier side sinks.

questions.question: What does “Pattern” mean?
options: Things arranged so a part repeats in order
The repeating chunk, e.g. AB or ABC
The same unit comes again and again
Gets bigger each step (1, 2, 3, 4…)
questions.answer: Things arranged so a part repeats in order
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Pattern means Things arranged so a part repeats in order.

questions.question: What does “Rule” mean?
options: The repeating chunk, e.g. AB or ABC
The same unit comes again and again
Gets bigger each step (1, 2, 3, 4…)
Will definitely happen / can never happen
questions.answer: The repeating chunk, e.g. AB or ABC
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Rule means The repeating chunk, e.g. AB or ABC.

questions.question: What does “Symmetry” mean?
options: When both halves of a shape match
The fold line where halves match
A flipped mirror image
Having matching halves
questions.answer: When both halves of a shape match
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Symmetry means When both halves of a shape match.

questions.question: What does “Line of symmetry / mirror line” mean?
options: The fold line where halves match
A flipped mirror image
Having matching halves
The way clock hands move
questions.answer: The fold line where halves match
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Line of symmetry / mirror line means The fold line where halves match.
